text: Hi friends, good evening. This is a case of simple laparoscopic stratum. This is not a colicitis. This is a patient with colic disease.
The surgeon begins the procedure by using a hook to address adhesions, starting dissection from the lateral aspect of the surgical field.
The surgeon observes vasodilation of the cystic duct and common bile duct (CBD) while immobilizing the duodenum from the cystic duct.

The surgeon carefully separates the peritoneum from the cystic duct, ensuring the dissection remains above the level of the Rouvière's sulcus. The peritoneum covering the gallbladder is gradually opened.
The surgeon uses a hook to carefully dissect the cystic duct, separating it fiber by fiber. The process involves skeletonizing the duct with low current monopolar cautery.
The surgeon approaches the cystic gland situated in Calot's triangle, identifying the node that serves as a landmark for the cystic artery.
The surgeon separates the peritoneum covering the node and opens the peritoneum over the catheter, progressing towards skeletonizing the cystic duct.
The surgeon uses a Maryland dissector to skeletonize the cystic artery within the dissected Calot's triangle, while the lower part of the gallbladder is removed from the liver bed.

The surgeon fully sterilizes the cystic duct, with the common bile duct (CBD) and duodenum clearly visualized and separated from the cystic duct. Clips are applied: three on the cystic duct, one between the stone, and one towards the cystic artery.
The surgeon coils and cuts the cystic artery above the previously placed clip, noting that pulsation becomes visible only after ligation.
The surgeon separates the gallbladder from the liver bed using a monopolar hook, stripping the peritoneum from the gallbladder in preparation for its removal.
This is a simple case of porous plaque cross-copy cholestectomy with a negligible blood loss.

The gallbladder is fully released from the liver bed and is suspended from the cystic duct. The gallbladder is separated from the cystic duct, ensuring that only one lumen is attached during the cutting process.
